_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Narrowing or blockage of the coronary arteries, usually caused by atherosclerosis (build-up of cholesterol and fatty deposits/plaques).
Symptoms such as angina (chest discomfort), pain in the arms or shoulders, shortness of breath, sweating, or dizziness.
Defined as an established pattern of angina pectoris, a history of myocardial infarction (MI), or the presence of plaque documented by catheterization.

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals and examination findings.
Investigation reports establishing diagnosis ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/ Angiography/ Holter?

claim.label: Relevant Investigations report ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/ Angiography/ Holter

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals and examination findings.

common_queries: Patient's ECG/ECHO/Cardiac enzymes/Angiography/Holter reports are not suggestive of the diagnosis.
Clinical notes detailing history and admission notes showing vitals and examination findings are missing or incomplete.
Investigation reports establishing diagnosis (ECG/ECHO/Cardiac Enzymes/Angiography/Holter) are missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Coronary artery disease (CAD) involves narrowing or blockage of coronary arteries, primarily due to atherosclerosis (plaque buildup).
Stable CAD is defined by an established pattern of angina pectoris, a history of myocardial infarction (MI), or the presence of plaque documented by catheterization.
Symptoms include angina (chest discomfort), pain in arms/shoulders, shortness of breath, sweating, and dizziness.
Diagnosis relies on ECG, ECHO, Tread Mill Testing (TMT), Coronary Angiogram (CAG), and CT Angiography.

Medical management aims to alleviate symptoms, prevent cardiovascular events, and reduce mortality, primarily with Lipid-lowering agents (Statins), Antihypertensive agents (Beta blockers, ACE inhibitors; alternatives: calcium channel blockers, ARBs), and Antiplatelet agents (Aspirin, Clopidogrel).
